parentQuestion: The dorsal body cavity contains the ___.

scaffolds.explain: DORSAL means BACK (posterior). In humans standing upright, dorsal structures are on your back side. Think: Dorsal fin on a shark.

wrongExplains.0: Front/belly side is ventral; dorsal is back.
wrongExplains.2: Side is lateral; dorsal refers to back.
wrongExplains.3: Head region is cranial; dorsal refers to back.

scaffolds.explain: VENTRAL means FRONT or BELLY SIDE. In humans, ventral structures are on the front (anterior) of the body.

wrongExplains.0: Back side is dorsal; ventral is front.
wrongExplains.2: Upper is superior; ventral refers to front/back position.
wrongExplains.3: Lower is inferior; ventral refers to front/back position.

scaffolds.q: What is the dorsal body cavity?
options: The cavity along the back containing the brain and spinal cord
scaffolds.explain: The DORSAL BODY CAVITY runs along the BACK and contains the BRAIN AND SPINAL CORD. It includes cranial and vertebral cavities.

wrongExplains.0: The chest cavity is thoracic (ventral cavity).
wrongExplains.2: The abdominal cavity is part of the ventral cavity.
wrongExplains.3: The pelvic cavity is part of the ventral cavity.

scaffolds.q: What is the central nervous system?
options: All the nerves in the body
scaffolds.explain: The CENTRAL NERVOUS SYSTEM (CNS) is the BRAIN AND SPINAL CORD — the command center. Everything else is peripheral.

wrongExplains.0: All body nerves include peripheral; CNS is brain and spinal cord only.
wrongExplains.3: Autonomic nerves are part of peripheral nervous system.

options: The cavity in the skull that contains the brain
scaffolds.explain: The CRANIAL CAVITY is in the SKULL and CONTAINS THE BRAIN. It's part of the dorsal body cavity.

wrongExplains.0: The chest cavity is thoracic; cranial is in the skull.
wrongExplains.3: The spinal canal is the vertebral cavity; cranial holds the brain.

options: The canal within the spine containing the spinal cord
scaffolds.explain: The VERTEBRAL CAVITY is the canal WITHIN THE SPINE that contains the SPINAL CORD. It's part of the dorsal body cavity.

wrongExplains.0: The brain is in the cranial cavity.

scaffolds.q: What protects the structures in the dorsal cavity?
options: Bone — the skull and vertebral column
scaffolds.explain: BONE protects the dorsal cavity structures — the SKULL protects the brain and the VERTEBRAL COLUMN protects the spinal cord.

wrongExplains.0: Hard bone provides the main protection for CNS.
wrongExplains.2: Bone is the primary protector; muscles provide secondary support.
wrongExplains.3: The diaphragm separates thoracic from abdominal cavities.

scaffolds.q: What is the relationship between dorsal cavity and CNS?
options: The dorsal cavity houses and protects the CNS
The CNS is in the ventral cavity
The dorsal cavity contains digestive organs
scaffolds.explain: The DORSAL CAVITY HOUSES AND PROTECTS THE CNS (brain and spinal cord). This critical nervous tissue is enclosed in bone.

wrongExplains.0: There's a direct relationship — dorsal cavity contains CNS.
wrongExplains.2: The CNS is in the dorsal cavity, not ventral.
wrongExplains.3: Digestive organs are in the ventral (abdominal) cavity.

scaffolds.q: The dorsal body cavity contains the ___?
options: Brain and spinal cord (central nervous system)
scaffolds.explain: The dorsal body cavity contains the BRAIN AND SPINAL CORD (CENTRAL NERVOUS SYSTEM). It runs along the back of the body.

wrongExplains.0: Heart and lungs are in the thoracic cavity (ventral).
wrongExplains.2: Stomach and intestines are in the abdominal cavity (ventral).
wrongExplains.3: Reproductive organs are in the pelvic cavity (ventral).
